meta.setting: French Gulch mining settlement, gulch road, stamp-mill noise, claims, saloons, cabins, and exhausted creek bottoms
meta.rdr_clone_target: RDR-clone western with mining-town pressure, road dust, debt, law trouble, and hard living

research_grounding.title: NPS Whiskeytown History and Culture
research_grounding.use: Gold rush settlement, mining, logging, ranching, immigrant labor, and changing land use near Whiskeytown.

research_grounding.title: California State Parks Shasta State Historic Park
research_grounding.use: Northern California gold-rush town texture, courthouse commerce, and mining-era settlement pressure.

generation_prompt.task: Generate the French Gulch region page for the 1899 Jefferson State western packet. This JSON contains shape and guidance only.
page_shape: H3 local marker line in the register of Stamp Mill, Creek Bed, and Main Street
square image reference immediately after the marker line
opening blockquote built as three short generated lines: hard object, road or weather detail, plain table meaning
two compact paragraphs establishing the gulch as settlement, job board, rumor mill, danger pocket, and moral pressure cooker
closing blockquote that gives a generated town sign and practical table meaning
generation_guidance: Generate a working mining settlement, not a postcard: ore wagons, boarding rooms, stamp-mill thump, bad credit, claim arguments, saloon heat, and creek mud.
Give the table ways to start scenes from wages owed, ore missing, law papers, travelers passing through, Native families trading nearby, and animals nosing through refuse at night.
Keep the town useful as a hub but never comfortable; every service should carry a price, a witness, or a complication.
Let the place smell of wet timber, whiskey, mule sweat, hot iron, lamp smoke, and creek rot.
generation_prompt.hand_cut_note: The gulch should sound like a place where every board in the sidewalk knows who lied yesterday.
generation_prompt.table_spark: A French Gulch scene should ask who came to buy, who came to hide, who came to collect, and what the creek gave up after rain.
must_preserve: The generated page must include image path regions/images/french-gulch.png.
The prompt must not contain final prose, final blockquotes, or finished field marks.
avoid: do not mention any prior packet, inherited taxonomy, or non-western source language
do not make the settlement clean, cute, legendary, or theme-park tidy
do not copy names, locations, missions, or characters from any commercial western game
self_check: The future markdown follows the exact heading sequence.
The future page is about a rough 1899 mining settlement with scene hooks, not finished lore.
The prompt contains no finished page prose.
All language belongs to grounded Jefferson State western play.
